pivot_score_rank: SIX in BibleWorld history (chain-probe 8.90, cot-fidelity 8.85, context-lens 8.80, prompt-shield 8.75, semantic-pass-k 8.65, context-trace 8.225, invariant-probe 8.175, session-lens 7.90)
key_patterns: PAT-070 — Genesis 7:1-24 (The Sealed Invariance Pattern, Level 3, score 8.5/10): The ark maintains total internal behavioral continuity while the external world undergoes total catastrophic state change. The measure of the ark's success is BEHAVIORAL INVARIANCE. Maps to AI agent behavioral invariance testing: when the environment around an AI agent changes (system time, OS variables, API latency, tool availability, connection strings), the agent's core behavior should remain invariant to irrelevant changes. invariant-probe implements InvarianceScore = mean cosine similarity across perturbation matrix vs. baseline output. No pip library currently does this.

PAT-071 — John 4:16-18 (The Hidden History Verification Pattern, Level 3, score 8.2/10): Jesus accesses the woman's full history without her providing it. The claim is specific, falsifiable, and verified ('five husbands' — a specific count, confirmed by the woman). Maps to session memory fidelity testing: inject ground truth session history as context, probe agent's recall of specific events, verify against ground truth, compute SessionMemoryFidelity. No pip library implements multi-turn session memory fidelity scoring.

PAT-073 — Daniel 3:26-27 (Furnace Attestation Protocol, Level 2, score 7.5/10): After maximum adversarial pressure, a multi-witness protocol produces zero-damage attestation at four independent surfaces (bodies, hair, robes, smell). Maps to post-task attestation for AI agents: after completing high-stakes operations, certify every system surface that should NOT have been modified. Absorbed into invariant-probe as 'iprobe attest' command.
pain_point_solved_primary: AI agents are not tested for behavioral invariance under irrelevant environmental perturbations. When connection strings change, system time shifts, API latency spikes, or tool availability changes — does the agent still behave the same way? The Claude Code database deletion incident (March 2026) is the canonical failure: the agent's behavior was NOT invariant to a connection string change, destroying a production database. Every major observability tool (Arize Phoenix, Langfuse, LangSmith, AgentPrism, Braintrust) records WHAT happened. Zero tools test whether the agent BEHAVES THE SAME across environmental perturbations. [WEB-FRESH 2026-03-31] Braintrust 2026 (7 top tools audited, none address behavioral invariance). YC RFS 2026 (testing and debugging gap). Anthropic research: 'new forms of post-deployment monitoring infrastructure required.'
pain_point_solved_secondary: Long-session AI agents accumulate history in context windows / memory stores. This history drives current behavior. But the history can be partially loaded (cache miss), incorrectly ordered (vector store retrieval errors), contradicted by new context (document updated), or hallucinated. Anthropic's cache bug (March 2026): tool schema bytes changing mid-session caused cache misses, inflating costs 10-20x. No tool verifies that the agent's session memory is ACCURATE relative to the ground truth history. [WEB-FRESH 2026-03-31] piunikaweb.com (cache bug documented), Anthropic API docs (prompt caching architecture).
competitive_moat_primary: GREEN [WEB-FRESH 2026-03-31] — Tools audited: Arize Phoenix (observability, NOT behavioral invariance), Langfuse (execution tracing, NOT behavioral invariance), AgentPrism (Evil Martians, 2026 — trace visualization, NOT behavioral invariance), Braintrust (evaluation, NOT behavioral invariance), LangSmith (LangChain tracing, NOT behavioral invariance), Hypothesis (property-based testing for DETERMINISTIC code, NOT stochastic AI agents). NONE produce InvarianceScore across environmental perturbation matrix for AI agents. Window: 4-6 months.
competitive_moat_secondary: GREEN [WEB-FRESH 2026-03-31] — Tools audited: RAGAS (single-turn RAG evaluation, NOT session memory fidelity), DeepEval groundedness (single-turn, NOT multi-turn session history), TruLens (5 metrics — relevance, groundedness, coherence, toxicity, answer relevance — NO session fidelity). NONE produce SessionMemoryFidelity scoring for multi-turn agent sessions. Window: 4-6 months.

invariant_probe_summary.core_api: InvariantSuite + EnvironmentMatrix (TimeShift, TemperatureShift, LatencyInjection, EnvVarMutation, ToolAvailabilityChange, ConnectionStringMutation) + InvarianceReport + AttestationReport + @invariance_probe decorator + CLI (iprobe run/show/gate/attest/estimate) + pytest plugin
invariant_probe_summary.key_innovation: First tool to provide InvarianceScore (behavioral invariance measure) for AI agents across environmental perturbation matrices. InvarianceScore = mean cosine similarity(all_perturbed_outputs, baseline_output). Also: iprobe attest (post-task zero-damage attestation, Daniel 3 pattern — certifies which system surfaces were verified unmodified). CI-gateable. LLM-agnostic.
invariant_probe_summary.implementation_status: API spec complete, sprint plan written (8 weeks), known unknowns documented (KU-053 through KU-056)
invariant_probe_summary.dependencies: sentence-transformers, anthropic/openai SDK, click, rich, numpy, pyyaml, pytest (optional)

session_lens_summary.core_api: HistoryProbe + SessionLens + MemoryReport (session_memory_fidelity, cache_miss_events, hallucinated_events, ordering_errors) + CLI (slens run/show/gate/export-probes) + pytest plugin
session_lens_summary.key_innovation: First tool to produce SessionMemoryFidelity score for multi-turn AI agent sessions — verifies agent's recalled history matches ground truth at the event level. Catches cache misses (Anthropic March 2026 bug), vector store ordering errors, context truncation, hallucinated recall. CI-gateable.
session_lens_summary.implementation_status: API spec complete, sprint plan written (6-7 weeks), known unknowns documented (KU-057 through KU-059)
session_lens_summary.dependencies: anthropic/openai SDK, sentence-transformers, click, rich, numpy, pyyaml, pytest (optional)

open_threads: KU-053: Optimal InvarianceScore threshold — at what threshold does behavioral drift become a reliability concern? Study needed.
KU-054: Interaction effects in perturbation matrix — does applying latency + env var mutation simultaneously produce different results than each alone? V0.1 tests independently.
KU-055: Embedding model sensitivity for InvarianceScore — does invariance_score change significantly across embedding models?
KU-056: Temperature = 0 invariance behavior — at T=0, agents are deterministic. What does invariance testing add?
KU-057: How to distinguish cache miss events from actual non-recall (agent genuinely doesn't process that history segment) vs. model limitation.
KU-058: Optimal probe question generation — what mix of recall/ordering/attribution/negation maximizes diagnostic coverage?
KU-059: Multi-document history — when session history spans multiple documents, how to handle cross-document attribution correctly?
KU-048: Optimal k for reliable attribution in context-trace — at what k does attribution_score[chunk] converge?
KU-049: Interaction effects in context-trace — removing chunks A and B together may differ from removing each separately.
KU-050: Embedding model sensitivity for context-trace — does attribution_score change significantly across embedding models?
KU-051: Temperature effect on context-trace attribution — T=0 vs T=0.7 attribution variance study needed.
KU-052: Optimal chunk granularity for context-trace — sub-paragraph vs. paragraph vs. document level.
KU-037: fault_score threshold calibration for chain-probe v0.1 — BLOCKING for CI gate claim

notes: Cycle 021 BIG_TECH_GAP_ANALYSIS produced TWO viable build candidates in a single cycle — first time this has happened. Genesis 7 (sealed invariance) and John 4:16-18 (hidden history verification) both generated Level 3 patterns with Pivot_Score >= 7.0. The Genesis 7 structural insight is counterintuitive but precise: the ark's success is measured not by survival but by BEHAVIORAL INVARIANCE of its contents — nothing inside the ark changed despite total external catastrophe. This reframes AI agent testing: the question is not 'did the agent succeed?' but 'did the agent behave the same across environmental perturbations?' — a question NO current tool answers. ENFORCEMENT IS OVERDUE (4 cycles since cycle 017). This must be the first task of cycle 022 before any new pattern work. Build priority: invariant-probe (8-week sprint). The window for the entire BibleWorld tool suite remains 4-6 months. [WEB-FRESH 2026-03-31] 8 searches run. Both GREEN statuses confirmed.
